movement-set.movement-set-description: Blocking Set I is the first set learned in American Kenpo Karate. This set consists of six blocking movements executed with the purpose of blocking a different set of attacks.

movement.movement-description: Upper block allows the user to defend against attack coming from above, for example, an attack with a club or a metal bar.
Inner block allows the user to defend against a direct attack to the face, or a circular attack.
Outer extended block allow you to defend against a circular attack.
Downward block allow you to block a frontal or circular low attack, for example, a kick.
While returning your arm to the hip, you are executing a lateral block, protecting you from an attack coming from your side.
